name: From Altea to Serra de Toix – Coastal Circular Walk
name_original: Desde Altea hasta Serra de Toix – Paseo circular por la costa
region_intro: Touring cycling around Altea offers diverse terrain, from coastal routes along the Mediterranean to challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Bernia and Sierra Helada mountain ranges, providing panoramic views and varied elevations. Inland, routes traverse river valleys, loquat orchards, and terraced fields. This geographical blend creates a range of cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Altea The most popular touring cycling route is From Altea to Serra de Toix – Coastal Circular Walk, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers coastal views and significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Algar Springs Waterfalls – Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) loop from Olla Altea, a difficult 37.7 miles (60.7 km) path. This route features a substantial climb and passes by the Algar River Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Altea Marina – Poniente Promenade loop from Altea, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and promenades, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Altea is defined by coastal routes, mountain ascents, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain challenges. The routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2,900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Altea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Altea?
A: Altea offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 60 options available on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Altea?
A: The mild climate of Altea makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for touring cycling. During these periods, temperatures are pleasant, and there's less tourist traffic, providing optimal conditions for exploring the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when cycling around Altea?
A: Altea's geographical location provides a diverse range of terrain. You can find flat coastal routes along the Mediterranean, challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra de Bernia and Sierra Helada, and inland routes winding through river valleys, loquat orchards, and terraced fields.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea offers several easy and family-friendly options. For instance, the route from Altea to Albir Playa is a safe, mostly flat 6-kilometer trail along the coast, segregated from traffic. The initial section of the Algar River Valley also provides a beautiful and safe cycling route, mostly flat and separated from traffic, ideal for families.

Q: What are some challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Experienced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenges. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952020719/from-altea-to-serra-de-toix-coastal-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Altea to Serra de Toix – Coastal Circular Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.8-mile (31.9 km) trail with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19464629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls – Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) loop from Olla Altea&lt;/a&gt;, which features a substantial climb and covers 37.7 miles (60.7 km).

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Altea?
A: Yes, many routes in Altea are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952020719/from-altea-to-serra-de-toix-coastal-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Altea to Serra de Toix – Coastal Circular Walk&lt;/a&gt;. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39555694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altea Marina – Poniente Promenade loop from Altea&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal areas and promenades.

Q: What natural features can I see along touring cycling routes in Altea?
A: You can explore diverse natural features. Routes often pass through the rugged Sierra de Bernia and Sierra Helada Natural Park, offering panoramic views. The Algar River Valley features lush riparian forests and loquat orchards, while coastal paths provide breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints to look out for on a touring cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can enjoy dramatic cliffside views in the Sierra Helada Natural Park, with potential sightings of the Balearic Islands on clear days. The iconic Peñón de Ifach rock in Calpe is a prominent landmark visible from many coastal routes. For stunning vistas, consider the route to the Albir Lighthouse or the challenging climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt;, known for its breathtaking viewpoints. Altea's Old Town also offers panoramic views from the Mirador de los Cronistas de España.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, the Algar River is known for its natural springs and refreshing waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19464629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls – Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) loop from Olla Altea&lt;/a&gt; is a route that passes by the Algar River Valley, offering a unique natural experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Altea?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse terrain, which offers everything from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there places to eat or rest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Altea's routes often pass through charming beaches, small coves, and traditional Spanish villages, providing plenty of spots for breaks. You'll find numerous beach cafés along the seafront promenade and opportunities to stop for refreshments in the villages encountered on inland routes.

Q: Where can I rent a bike in Altea?
A: Altea is a cycling-friendly town with several bike rental shops available. Many accommodations also offer bike rental services or can recommend local providers, making it easy to find suitable touring bicycles for your adventures.
tips: A beautiful pebble beach at the end of the Altea promenade. The small cycle path towards the hinterland can be used (always) along the riverbed of the Riu Algar, and later along the Riu Guadalest. After heavy, persistent rainfall, the "small" stream turns into a raging river and the Cap Negret beach turns into a delta.

Sierra de Toix is the exit to the sea from the Sierra de Bernia and physical separation between the bay of Calpe (north) and the Altea (south).

The contrast on both sides of the bar is spectacular. The northern zone (Umbria) is dense in a blanket of villas and other buildings, many of them abandoned where there was a forest of conifers. While the south side (sunny side), as seen in the image above, peeled off due to strong solar radiation and a fire received in 2013.

The Mirador de la Plaza de la Iglesia (Church Square Viewpoint) in Altea is one of the most emblematic spots in the old town and offers one of the best panoramic views of the Costa Blanca.
It is located in the Plaza de la Iglesia, in the highest part of Altea's old town, next to the Church of Our Lady of Consolation, famous for its blue and white domes, known as the "Dome of the Mediterranean."
On clear days, you can see the skyline of Benidorm and the Peñón de Ifach (Ifach Rock) in Calpe.
